narrative:
DRIVER
GBP leads H4 CSM at 100 with USD close behind at 95, both accelerating off the D1 regime transition, driven by Fed hawkish repricing on persistent inflation and Iran-deal risk premium reduction pulling safe-haven flows out of CHF and JPY. GBPUSD H4 momentum at 70 rising with GBPJPY correlated at +0.89 and H4 1212 at 78 makes GBPJPY the cleanest expression, while USDCHF D1 and H4 both print Buy with CHF CSM at zero confirming the short CHF leg. Gold at 4537 up 0.7 percent contradicts the risk-on read and flags residual defensive positioning that caps USD upside on USDJPY.

MOMENTUM
Gaining: GBPJPY H4 1212 at 78 rising sharply, NZDUSD H4 rising off low base
Fading: USDCAD D1 fading from 76 as H4 drops to 53, AUDUSD D1 at 48 falling with H4 flat

WATCH
ECB June hike expectations firm, EURUSD sells into any EUR bounce with D1 strong sell and H4 sell aligned
WTI collapse at minus 9.35 percent hits USDCAD, further CAD strength triggers USDCAD breakdown toward D1 buy invalidation
Fed speakers referencing persistent inflation confirm USDJPY D1 buy resumption if H4 momentum at 58 stabilises

themes.theme: Iran-US peace negotiations reducing geopolitical risk premium
Fed hawkish shift on persistent inflation pressures
US Treasury yields rising with 20-year bonds at 5.122%
ECB rate hike expectations strengthening for June
Risk sentiment improving on Iran deal hopes and lower yields
Safe haven demand declining as geopolitical tensions ease
Central bank divergence between Fed hawkish and BoE dovish signals
EM weakness from negative sentiment and real rate headwinds
Commodity price pressure from abundant supplies and demand concerns

key_observation: Mixed signals for USD despite near-term strength. Fed hawkishness and rising yields support USD, but geopolitical risk reduction from Iran peace talks and BoE dovishness create headwinds. Risk appetite improving as safe havens sell off. Near-term USD strength constrained by narrowing yield differentials with ECB likely hiking in June.
watch: Fed minutes timing and inflation language, Iran deal progress updates, EUR/USD 1.1600 support level, AUD employment data impact, ECB June meeting confirmation, crude oil inventory trends, Nvidia earnings guidance for risk sentiment confirmation
